content_preview.title: X-energy Raises $1 Billion in Nasdaq Debut, Shares Surge 36 Percent in Record Nuclear IPO as Amazon-Backed SMR Maker Eyes First Reactor in the 2030s
content_preview.summary: X-energy priced its IPO at $23 a share on April 24, raising $1.02 billion — the largest nuclear IPO on record — as shares surged 36% on debut.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

X-energy, Inc. began trading on the Nasdaq Global Select Market on April 24, 2026, under the ticker symbol "XE," raising $1.02 billion in what [Invezz](https://invezz.com/news/2026/04/24/x-energy-surges-36-in-debut-as-1b-ipo-signals-nuclear-revival/) described as the largest nuclear IPO on record. Shares opened at $30.11 — a 36 percent gain over the $23 offering price — and reached an intraday high of $31.33, valuing the Rockville, Maryland-based small modular reactor developer at a...

recommendations: Before merging, the human reviewer should write a corrections file at src/content/corrections/<YYYY-MM>/<article-slug>.json documenting the warning(s).
Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured 851-word News article covering X-energy's April 2026 Nasdaq IPO with good section organization (Overview, What We Know, Technology, Ownership, Projects, Financial Profile, Analysis). Clear, factual reporting with appropriate context connecting the IPO to the broader clean energy IPO wave.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 4 sources read from disk snapshots. source-0 (x-energy.com IPO pricing PR): confirmed $23/share, 44,254,659 shares, April 24 trading start, J.P. Morgan/Morgan Stanley/Jefferies/Moelis underwriters, 30-day overallotment option, 'most robust nuclear fuel on earth' DOE quote. source-1 (x-energy.com IPO launch PR): confirmed $16-$19 initial range, April 15 launch date. source-2 (Invezz): confirmed Clay Sell quotes verbatim ('This is a validation of our nuclear technology...', 'We want to make nuclear boring...', 'I think they were impressed...'), 15x oversubscription, $700M original target, Ark $105M commitment, Ghaffarian 61% Class B, Ares 26%, financial losses ($390M net loss on $94M revenue). source-3 (MIT Technology Review): confirmed $11.5B market cap, 80MW per Xe-100 reactor, Plant Vogtle comparison, Amazon ~20% ownership, Dow Texas environmental approval, NRC pending, 2023 aborted IPO, AI-driven electricity demand context.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: One factual error identified: the Overview states 'Shares opened at $30.11 — a 36 percent gain over the $23 offering price.' Per Invezz (source-2), $30.11 represents approximately 31% above the $23 IPO price; the 36% figure refers to the intraday high of $31.33. The article incorrectly maps the 36% figure to the opening price rather than the session high. All other claims, figures, and quotes verified against their cited sources.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional throughout. No sensationalism or loaded language. The 'What We Don't Know' section appropriately acknowledges uncertainty about IPO proceeds and NRC timeline, adding editorial balance.
editor_notes.originality: No dedicated X-energy IPO article exists in the archive. A prior April 28 article on Fervo Energy's IPO filing mentioned X-energy in passing. This is original, substantive coverage.
concerns: Opening price gain (31%) conflated with intraday session gain (36%): article states 'opened at $30.11 — a 36 percent gain' but $30.11/$23 = ~31%; 36% applies to the $31.33 intraday high per Invezz. A corrections record has been filed.
recommendations: Add x-energy.com, invezz.com, and technologyreview.com to source allowlist for future submissions
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Substantively accurate and well-reported article with one minor numerical misattribution (31% open vs. 36% session high). Corrections record filed. Approved for publication with corrections.
